stories.headline: Hacker used Claude to break into the ticketing system for Lollapalooza, Bonnaroo, and hundreds more festivals
stories.blurb: A security researcher paired with Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 to find vulnerabilities in Front Gate, the ticketing platform used by nearly every major US music festival. He could issue himself any ticket he wanted. The researcher reported the flaw, which has since been patched.
stories.why_it_matters: AI coding assistants now help find security holes faster than companies can patch them.
stories.original_headline: Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival

stories.headline: Anthropic is talking to Samsung about building custom AI chips
stories.blurb: About a week after OpenAI announced its own custom chip deal with Broadcom, Anthropic is now in discussions with Samsung to develop specialized hardware for running its models. Both AI labs are moving beyond renting Nvidia GPUs toward controlling their own silicon.
stories.why_it_matters: The race to own the hardware stack is heating up fast.
stories.original_headline: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung

stories.headline: Google DeepMind just partnered with A24 to study AI in filmmaking
stories.blurb: The indie studio behind Everything Everywhere All at Once is teaming up with DeepMind to explore how AI tools could fit into actual film production. They'll work together on research projects and give filmmakers early access to Google's AI models to see what works in real creative workflows.
stories.why_it_matters: First major AI lab partnering with a respected studio to test tools with actual filmmakers, not just demos.
stories.original_headline: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership

stories.headline: Meta just launched an app that turns your text into playable games
stories.blurb: Meta quietly released Pocket, an experimental app that generates interactive mini games from text prompts. You type what you want—'a maze where you're a cat chasing mice' or 'Flappy Bird but underwater'—and the AI builds a playable game you can share. It's live now as a standalone app.
stories.why_it_matters: Game creation just became as easy as texting—no code, no design skills required.
stories.original_headline: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket

try_this_prompt.title: The assumption audit
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm making a strategic decision I'll describe below. List every assumption I'm making that has to be true for this to work. Then pick the two most dangerous ones and tell me the cheapest possible test I could run this week to validate or kill each assumption.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you commit budget, political capital, or six months to a plan built on hope.

daily_question: If you could instantly turn any idea into a working prototype with AI, what would you build first?
